title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four amateur mechanics who restored a vintage vehicle. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: Old Metal, New Life: The Restorers

texts.A:
### Marco, The 1960s Scooter
'I wanted something stylish and simple, or so I thought,' Marco laughs. [51]The hardest part was the hunt for original bolts, which took months of scouring obscure Italian websites.[/51] [48]I was surprised to find that my general patience and attention to detail in my job as a programmer improved immensely.[/48] [53]I learned that restoration is a form of respect for the designers of the past.[/53] [50]I realized that my background in electronics helped me troubleshoot a persistent spark plug issue that baffled others.[/50] [47]Initially, the smell of petrol and oil in my small garage was quite overwhelming and lingering.[/47] It was a labor of love.
texts.B:
### Sarah, The Vintage Campervan
Sarah restored a van to travel across Europe. 'It was my ticket to freedom,' she explains. [49]The experience taught me that we are far more capable of fixing our own lives than we are led to believe.[/49] [52]I hadn't anticipated the deep emotional attachment I’d feel to a machine; it felt like a member of the family.[/52] [47]I was quite startled by how much the constant kneeling and reaching affected my knees in the first month.[/47] [54]What I valued most was the online community of 'van-lifers', who provided technical tips at 3 a.m.[/54] [56]If I hadn't found the van abandoned in a neighbor's field, I’d never have started the project.[/56] It was a life-changing journey.
texts.C:
### Victor, The Classic Tractor
Victor restored a machine from his grandfather’s farm. [51]We struggled with the sheer weight of the components; every task required heavy lifting equipment and multiple people.[/51] [48]I noticed that my physical strength and stamina reached an all-time high, which was a great side effect.[/48] [53]I realized that these machines were built to last forever, unlike the disposable technology of today.[/53] [50]I found that the discipline I used in my previous career as a logistics manager was perfect for sourcing rare engine parts.[/50] [55]It took a while to adjust to the grease and grime, which I found quite repulsive at first.[/55] [52]I’d initially worried it was too far gone to save.
texts.D:
### Elena, The Retro Bicycle
'I wanted to understand the mechanics of motion,' Elena says. [49]The project made me realize that modern convenience often hides the beautiful simplicity of mechanical engineering.[/49] [56]I wouldn't have found the right parts if an elderly neighbor hadn't shared his personal collection of vintage tools.[/56] [54]I was moved by the interest of local cycling enthusiasts, who stopped to offer advice and admire the progress.[/54] [47]I hadn't expected the mental fatigue of following complex diagrams to be so draining initially.[/47] [55]I found the initial task of removing decades of rust incredibly tedious and frustrating.[/55] [52]I’d been concerned that I wasn't 'technical' enough, but the bicycle was a patient teacher.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by the physical or environmental impact of the restoration work?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) mentions the smell of petrol, Sarah (B) the impact on her knees, and Elena (D) the mental fatigue of diagrams.

questions.question: notes a positive physical or professional improvement that resulted from the project?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) saw his programming patience improve, and Victor (C) saw his strength reach an all-time high.

questions.question: expresses a change in their perspective on personal capability or modern technology?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) reflects on fixing our own lives, and Elena (D) on the simplicity hidden by modern convenience.

questions.question: mentions how their professional background or skills were advantageous to the work?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) used his electronics background, and Victor (C) his logistics management skills.

questions.question: describes a specific challenge related to sourcing parts or physical weight?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) mentions the hunt for original bolts, and Victor (C) the weight of components.

questions.question: admits to having had prior doubts or having formed an unexpected emotional bond?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) felt a deep attachment to the machine, and Elena (D) worried she wasn't 'technical' enough.

questions.question: reflects on a broader philosophical lesson learned about durability or respect for design?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) learned respect for past designers, and Victor (C) realized machines were built to last.

questions.question: highlights the value of the advice or support from a specific group or community?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) valued the 'van-lifers' community, and Elena (D) the local cycling enthusiasts.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration or adjustment to the unpleasant aspects of the task?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) adjusted to the grease/grime, and Elena (D) found removing rust tedious.

questions.question: acknowledges the specific external source or find that initiated the project?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) found the van in a neighbor's field, and Elena (D) used tools from an elderly neighbor.
